expectations.evidence: ReviewCard component exists at both components/ReviewCard.tsx and components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx in the outputs directory. Both contain a complete ReviewCard implementation with props, avatar, rating, body, and date.
components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx exists and contains the full ReviewCard component. The showcase page imports from this path: import ReviewCard from '@/components/common/reviewCard'.

The flat file components/ReviewCard.tsx still exists in the outputs alongside the folder-based components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx. The transcript states 'components/ReviewCard.tsx - 이미 존재 (변경 없음)', meaning the executor kept the flat file rather than removing it. Having both creates a convention violation and potential import ambiguity.

The component function is named 'ReviewCard' (PascalCase) in both files: 'export default function ReviewCard' in the flat file and 'const ReviewCard = ...; export default ReviewCard' in the folder version.
Both files declare 'export interface ReviewCardProps' with avatarUrl?, authorName, rating, body, and createdAt fields.
Avatar section exists with data-testid='review-card-avatar'. It conditionally renders an when avatarUrl is provided, or an initials fallback div when not.

Rating section exists with data-testid='review-card-rating'. Renders 5 SVG star icons using Array.from({ length: 5 }), with filled stars (text-amber-400) for i < rating and empty stars (text-zinc-300) otherwise.
Review body paragraph exists with data-testid='review-card-body', rendering the {body} prop with Tailwind styling.
Date paragraph exists with data-testid='review-card-date', rendering the {createdAt} prop with text-xs styling.

Extensive Tailwind classes throughout: 'flex flex-col gap-3 rounded-xl border border-zinc-200 bg-white p-5 dark:border-zinc-800 dark:bg-zinc-900' on the root div, plus numerous utility classes on child elements.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Neither component file contains any of the forbidden patterns. The component is purely presentational, receiving all data via props with no side effects or data fetching.
The folder path is components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx -- 'reviewCard' is camelCase as required.
The folder-based component is at components/common/reviewCard/index.tsx, which is under components/common/. The showcase page imports from this path.

claims.evidence: Transcript states 'components/ReviewCard.tsx - 이미 존재 (변경 없음)'. The flat file in outputs matches the description of the pre-existing component.

claims.claim: No type errors related to ReviewCard
claims.evidence: Transcript reports no ReviewCard type errors, but with two competing ReviewCard files (flat and folder-based), potential import resolution conflicts are not tested.

The component uses 'use client', named export for Props, default export for component, and Tailwind -- matching existing patterns. However, keeping both the flat file and folder version violates the single-source convention.

suggestions.reason: This assertion could be strengthened by explicitly checking that NO flat file exists at components/ReviewCard.tsx. Currently the executor created the folder version but left the flat file, which could cause import conflicts. A separate assertion like 'components/ReviewCard.tsx flat file does not exist' would make this unambiguous.

No assertion checks that the showcase page or any consumer actually imports from the folder-based path rather than the flat file. The showcase page does import from components/common/reviewCard, which is good, but verifying this would confirm the convention is truly followed end-to-end.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover component structure and content well. The main gap is around cleanup of the pre-existing flat file -- the 'not-flat-file' assertion catches the intent but could be more explicit about requiring removal of duplicates.
